findings.message: The client now sends a new query parameter `include_location=true`, but the committed consumer pact in tests/functional/test_consumer_inventory.py declares the interaction as `with_request("GET", "/inventory/1")` with no query parameters. The published pact therefore won't document that the consumer now calls the endpoint with `include_location=true`, so provider verification has no way to confirm the provider honors that parameter. Regenerate/republish the pact (and update the consumer test's `with_request` to include the query param) so the published contract matches the client's actual request shape.
findings.suggestion: Add the query parameter to the consumer interaction, e.g. `.with_query_parameter("include_location", "true")`, then re-run the consumer test to regenerate pacts/demo-inventory.json and republish.
